text: The x-middleware-subrequest header is spoofable (CVE-2025-29927). Do optimistic session-presence checks in proxy.ts only; perform authorization in the DAL, close to the data.
title: proxy.ts is not an authorization boundary (post-CVE-2025-29927)

text: Wrapping an auth function as (select auth.uid()) causes Postgres to run an initPlan and cache the result per-statement rather than per-row (over 100x on large tables). Every exposed table gets ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
title: RLS performance: wrap auth.uid() in (select ...) for an initPlan; FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY

text: getClaims() verifies the JWT against the project JWKS (usually locally for asymmetric keys). getSession() reads values directly from storage (cookies) which may not be authentic; it is insecure on the server.
title: getClaims() verifies the JWT; getSession() is unverified server-side

text: Inside a 'use cache' boundary you cannot access runtime APIs such as cookies(), headers(), or searchParams. Read these values outside the cached scope and pass them in as explicit arguments so tenant/user IDs join the cache key.
title: Cached functions cannot read cookies()/headers()/searchParams

text: revalidatePath purges the cached data for a route after a mutation so the next request renders fresh data (read-your-writes). Call it from a Server Action or Route Handler after a write; pair with revalidateTag for tag-scoped invalidation.
title: revalidatePath / revalidateTag: invalidate cached routes after a write

text: redirect() throws a special error to short-circuit rendering and send the user elsewhere; in Server Components/Actions use it after an authz check fails. It must be called outside try/catch that swallows the control-flow error.
title: redirect() control-flow in Server Components and Actions

text: Row security policies restrict which rows a command can return or modify. FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY also applies policies to the table owner. Policies are per-command (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE); USING filters reads, WITH CHECK validates writes.
title: PostgreSQL row security policies; FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; USING vs WITH CHECK

text: SECURITY DEFINER functions execute with the privileges of the function owner, not the caller. Set a safe search_path and grant narrowly; they are the controlled escape hatch for RLS-bypassing reads.
title: CREATE FUNCTION SECURITY DEFINER runs as the owner; pin search_path

text: A custom access token hook injects authorization claims (role, cohort) into the JWT from app_metadata, not user_metadata. Read auth/cohort data from auth.jwt() / app_metadata; user_metadata is user-editable and must never drive authz.
title: Supabase custom access token hook: authz claims from app_metadata, never user_metadata

text: supabase gen types typescript generates TypeScript types from the database schema. Regenerate and commit after every migration; a CI drift check (gen vs committed) catches schema changes that were not reflected in the types.
title: Supabase generated TypeScript types and drift checking

text: Formula/CSV injection: a cell beginning with = + - @ or tab/CR can be executed as a formula by a spreadsheet program. Sanitize exported fields (prefix with a single quote or strip leading operators) before emitting CSV.
title: OWASP CSV (formula) injection on data export

text: WCAG contrast: normal text needs a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (AA) or 7:1 (AAA); large text and UI components need at least 3:1. Ratios are computed from sRGB relative luminance; 4.499:1 does not pass 4.5:1.
title: WCAG colour contrast minimums (4.5:1 / 3:1 / 7:1)

text: Verify webhook signatures with the endpoint secret before trusting the event; reconstruct the signed payload and compare. Never resolve privileged state from an unverified webhook body. Use the raw request body for verification.
title: Stripe webhook signature verification

text: supabase.functions.invoke() sends the caller's session access token automatically: the supabase-js client wraps fetch with fetchWithAuth, which sets Authorization: Bearer <access token> on every request (including Edge Function invocations) unless the header is already set. Verified in @supabase/supabase-js 2.108.2 dist (fetchWithAuth -> headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${accessToken}`)).
title: supabase-js functions.invoke — automatic Authorization bearer forwarding

text: supabase.auth.mfa.listFactors() returns { all, totp, phone }: `all` holds every enrolled factor for the user; the `totp` bucket contains only VERIFIED TOTP factors (factor_type totp AND status verified); `phone` likewise verified phone factors. The listing is served by GoTrue from the authenticated user's record (server-verified), not from client state.
title: Supabase JS reference — auth.mfa.listFactors (verified-only totp bucket)

text: Zod 4 API - ID formats: z.uuid() validates RFC 9562/4122 UUIDs (with version-pinning variants like z.uuid({ version: 'v4' }) / z.uuidv4()); distinct from the looser z.guid(). Documented under Ids on the API page (anchor #uuids).
title: Zod 4 API - uuid validation (z.uuid)

text: RFC 9116 (A File Format to Aid in Security Vulnerability Disclosure): the security.txt file MUST be served at /.well-known/security.txt over HTTPS (section 3, Location & retrieval); it MUST contain a Contact field and an Expires field (sections 2.5.3/2.5.5); a redirect to an unrelated resource (e.g. a login page) fails the retrieval requirement.
title: RFC 9116: security.txt at /.well-known/security.txt with Contact + Expires

text: INSERT with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE requires UPDATE privilege on the table (checked at plan time, even when no conflict fires); ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING needs only INSERT — the append-only shape for revoked-UPDATE tables.
title: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT privilege requirements

text: Postgres ISO-style datetime output serializes timestamptz with a signed numeric UTC offset (e.g. +00:00), never the 'Z' suffix JS toISOString emits — stamp equality must compare instants, not strings.
title: Postgres ISO datetime output format

text: On proxies that APPEND to X-Forwarded-For, the left-most hop is client-supplied and spoofable; only the last hop added by a trusted proxy is reliable for rate-limit keying.
title: X-Forwarded-For spoofability caveat

text: On Vercel, x-real-ip and x-forwarded-for are both platform-sanitized to the real client IP (external hops stripped); x-real-ip is the guaranteed single-value form.
title: Vercel request headers: x-real-ip / x-forwarded-for sanitization

text: API4:2023 — public endpoints that trigger billable third-party work (SMS, STT, tokens) must be rate-limited and duration/size-capped or they are a denial-of-wallet vector.
title: OWASP API4:2023 Unrestricted Resource Consumption

text: z.url() validates protocol ONLY when the protocol option is passed (z.url({ protocol: /^https?$/ })); bare z.url() accepts any scheme, javascript: included.
title: Zod URL validation protocol option

text: Supabase database advisors: lint 0001_unindexed_foreign_keys flags FK columns without a covering index; policy-predicate FK columns should be indexed.
title: Supabase database advisors (0001 unindexed foreign keys)

text: APG Grid pattern: interactive composite rows need role=grid/row/gridcell with roving tabindex and aria-selected; a bare focusable div exposes no role or selection state to AT.
title: WAI-ARIA APG Grid (composite rows)

text: WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions: required-field status must be stated at the field (label/instruction), not only implied by a disabled control.
title: WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions

text: Deterministic hooks are the enforcement layer; the Stop gate defines done; provenance is verified against a pinned corpus; the gate config is harness-protected and mirrored in CI.
title: Agent-harness doctrine
